Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods explicitly use P. polyphylla var. yunnanensis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: N. benthamiana is explicitly the transient expression host.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: UGT80A41 was part of the co-expression sets that produced dioscin in N. benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Dioscin is discussed as a steroidal saponin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper says Paridis saponins have notable pharmacological/antitumor activities, but it does not explicitly state in Results that dioscin itself is antitumor in this engineering context.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: UGT80A41 was included in productive reconstructed pathways for de novo dioscin production; however its necessity as 'required' is inferred from pathway design rather than individually knocked out.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: UGT80A41 is named as a UGT gene, i.e. a UDP-glycosyltransferase.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed uses UGT80A41 as a known pathway component and does not newly biochemically characterize it here.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by localization imaging in N. benthamiana and transient pathway co-expression with metabolite profiling.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: tPpHMGR was part of co-expression sets producing dioscin.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state engineering HMGR localization to the ER might enhance biosynthesis, and co-expression with cholesterol biosynthetic genes including tPpHMGR significantly increased dioscin production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Dioscin production was measured in engineered N. benthamiana leaves.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability gives GenBank accession no. PP937144 for UGT73DY2.

Source_Species.reason: UGT73DY2 was identified from P. polyphylla var. yunnanensis.

Applied_Species.reason: UGT73DY2 was studied both as a gene from P. polyphylla var. yunnanensis and transiently expressed in N. benthamiana.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper identifies UGT73DY2 as a 4′-O-rhamnosyltransferase acting on steroidal diglycosides.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state it clusters in the UGT73 family; UGTs are family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferases with PSPG motif.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It is directly placed in Paridis/steroidal saponin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results show it catalyzes sugar-chain elongation from steroidal diglycosides to triglycosides.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It acts after 2′-O-rhamnosylation, a downstream/late sugar-chain elongation step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Polyphyllins V and VI with UDP-Rha were directly tested and converted.

Primary_Product.reason: Products identified as dioscin and pennogenin 3-O-β-chacotrioside.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Explicitly described in Abstract and Results.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: UGT73DY2 directly forms dioscin from polyphyllin V and forms pennogenin 3-O-β-chacotrioside from polyphyllin VI.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: It is clearly an important late glycosylation step, but the paper does not directly establish it as rate-limiting.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Dioscin and pennogenin 3-O-β-chacotrioside are direct products in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results include antitumor assays for products 3 and 4; the paper also states Paridis saponins have pharmacological activity.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Direct enzymatic conversion is shown, and engineered variants improved heterologous dioscin yield.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Engineered N. benthamiana leaves producing dioscin is supported, but 'P. polyphylla roots and buds show high pathway-gene expression' is not equivalent to metabolite accumulation site.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Candidate selection section states selected genes showed high expression in roots and buds and in 1- to 3-year-old plants.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: UGT73DY2 was transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results explicitly state UGT73DY2-YFP localized predominantly in both nucleus and ER.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Both in vitro enzyme assays and transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression are described.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S-YFP vector used for localization and pEAQ-HT for transient expression.

Omics_Data.reason: Data availability lists PRJNA1145501 and PRJNA1145321.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results/Methods, including transcriptomics, recombinant assays, MS/NMR, kinetics, mutagenesis, localization, and pathway reconstruction.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point matches Results: first characterized 4′-O-rhamnosyltransferase; products 3 and 4 formed from 1 and 2 using UDP-Rha; strict specificity including no activity on trillin; pathway order inferred; mutants A132T/A342V/I358T improved activity and I358T increased dioscin to 3.12 ± 0.11 μg/g DW.

Other_Important_Info.reason: All listed details are present: activity on Ophiopogon compound 8 to form 9; H20/D126 implicated; F101D lost activity; KM values 45.27 and 80.04 μM.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: No growth tradeoff is reported, and Discussion explicitly notes transient expression may affect quantitative reliability.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper presents UGT73DY2 and activity-enhanced variants as useful for synthetic biology production of dioscin and related triglycosides.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It forms the immediate precursor class required for UGT73DY2.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: It is important upstream, but rate-limiting/key-control status is not directly demonstrated here.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Its inclusion in reconstructed pathways links it to dioscin production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Engineered N. benthamiana leaves are supported; natural roots/buds claim is based on pathway-gene expression rather than direct metabolite accumulation.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: UGT73CE1-YFP localized predominantly in both nucleus and ER.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated co-expression in N. benthamiana leaves is described.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S-YFP localization construct and pEAQ-HT transient expression vector are supported by Methods.
